data.description: A presentation by the company will showcase research findings on three primary drug candidates developed using their innovative TRBC platform technology.

data.title: ATDev, based in Orange, receives $3 million Seed investment to expand assistive mobility offerings. #AT #AI #Funding
data.description: A company based in Orange, California named Assistive Technology Development, Inc. (ATDev) recently disclosed that they secured $3 million in Seed funding on October 16, 2025. ATDev specializes in AI-powered rehabilitation and personal mobility solutions. The funding round was headed by Dobrzelecki Legacy, highlighting investor interest in the company's innovative technologies and potential for growth.

data.title: Chartis Research names SAS a leader in 7 credit risk management categories, improving performance for financial services firms.
data.description: In the financial services sector, SAS has been recognized by Chartis Research as a Category Leader across seven sectors within the credit risk management market. This acknowledgment underscores SAS' commitment to providing a platform that is agile, efficient, and compliance-driven. The report was released on October 16, 2025, and highlights SAS' role in helping financial firms enhance their performance and decision-making processes. This recognition further solidifies SAS' position as a key player in the industry, demonstrating its strength in delivering innovative solutions.

data.title: Lightshift Energy and KeyBanc Capital Markets secure $75M facility for battery projects in US East Coast.
data.description: Lightshift Energy, a company specializing in battery energy storage projects in the U.S., and KeyBanc Capital Markets, the investment banking branch of KeyCorp, have finalized a $75 million credit facility. The funding will aid Lightshift in expanding their energy storage projects along the East Coast. This flexible facility comprises a term loan and construction-to-term loan. KeyCorp, listed on the NYSE as KEY, is based in Cleveland.

data.title: GCC colocation data center market is booming with Saudi Arabia leading, holding 80% of total power capacity. Existing capacity is 870 MW, upcoming expected to reach 4 GW by 2025. Around $3.1 billion in new investments forecasted.
data.description: A new database, "GCC Existing & Upcoming Data Center Portfolio," is now available on ResearchAndMarkets.com. Saudi Arabia leads the upcoming data center market in the GCC with nearly 80% of the total power capacity. The current data center capacity in the GCC stands at over 870 MW, while the upcoming capacity in the region is projected to reach approximately 4 GW. By the end of 2025, an estimated 134 MW of extra power capacity is anticipated to be added. The sector is set to attract around $3.1 billion in new investments.

data.title: Corpay meets regulatory requirements for acquiring Alpha Group International PLC, gaining FCA, MFSA, and Italian FDI Authority approval. (Characters: 112)
data.description: Corpay, Inc. (NYSE: CPAY) has declared that all regulatory prerequisites for the acquisition of Alpha Group International PLC have been met. Alpha's shareholder approval was attained on September 22, 2025. The acquisition has now also received approval from the FCA, MFSA, and the Italian FDI Authority. The finalization of the acquisition is still pending.

data.title: Propel Software predicts 5 key business and tech trends for 2026, including the resurgence of SaaS and AI integration.
data.description: Propel Software, a company based in Redwood City, California, has released its annual business and technology trends for 2026. The company, known for creating the first product value management (PVM) platform, predicts five key areas that will be significant for the upcoming year. CEO Ross Meyercord highlights that in 2026, SaaS continues to thrive alongside AI agents. The winning strategy will involve integrating the flexibility of AI agents with the convenience of SaaS applications. This fusion of technologies is projected to shape the business landscape in the coming year.
Fed's Miran: 25 bps cuts is making adjustment slower than needs to be.

data.title: Johnson & Johnson faces UK lawsuit by 3,000 people over asbestos-contaminated baby powder, alleging risks not disclosed since 1960s.
data.description: A legal claim involving 3,000 individuals has been filed against Johnson & Johnson in the UK, accusing them of knowingly selling asbestos-contaminated baby powder. The lawsuit, brought by KP Law, references internal memos and scientific reports and asserts that the powder contained fibrous talc, tremolite, and actinolite - all forms of asbestos linked to potentially fatal cancers. Allegations suggest that the company was aware of these risks as far back as the 1960s but did not provide any warnings on their product packaging. This comes following a large settlement in the US. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.
Fed's Miran: The policy has gotten about 1.5% points tighter this year, due to rapid rise in the neutral rate.

data.title: Homes.com report: U.S. home prices rose 2% in September, median price now $385,000, up $7,000 from last year.
data.description: A leading online residential marketplace, Homes.com under CoStar Group, has published a fresh report discussing home price trends in September. The report, based on current data collection, offers insights into price trends among major metros and house types. Initial data indicates national home prices saw a sustained increase in September, with the median home price climbing by 2% year-over-year. The median price reached $385,000, marking a $7,000 increase from the previous September.

data.title: Superteam Wheels partners with GCN en Español for 3 years to expand global brand. Strengthening their collaboration in GIRONA, Spain.
data.description: In GIRONA, Spain, on October 16, 2025, Superteam Wheels, a top Chinese manufacturer of carbon fiber bicycle wheelsets, revealed a significant three-year strategic alliance with GCN Spanish channel (GCN en Español). This partnership comes in the wake of a previous successful collaboration with the GCN.
